mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals weakening labor demand and declining consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based economic strength and robust consumer demand, which drives corporate profit growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an accuracy above 55%, or the interaction term loses explanatory power when added to a baseline regression model.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and cause immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns do not materialize following INDPRO declines in late-cycle periods, or the predictive coefficient remains statistically insignificant across multiple late-cycle episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0 percent, higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions reduce equity valuations and corporate investment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the threshold model fails to outperform a linear specification in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market and expanding household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in new housing starts reflect rising consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which stimulate related supply chains and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with a win rate above 52%, or the signal shows no predictive power in a walk-forward validation framework.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production following a post-shock contraction indicates restored manufacturing capacity and easing supply bottlenecks, which improves corporate output expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns do not significantly follow INDPRO rebounds in recovery regimes, or the hypothesis is rejected when the signal fails to achieve a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in backtested recovery periods.
